title: Today's AI Brief: This is your laptop… on AI
summary: A look at Nvidia's plan to reboot your PC with AI, TSMC's struggles to keep up with AI demand, and more.
imageAlt: Image from theverge.com for This is your laptop… on AI
imageCaption: This is your laptop… on AI (theverge.com)

body.text: I'm looking at some big moves in the world of AI. It seems like we're on the cusp of a platform shift, with AI capabilities being integrated directly into our devices. This is more than just a software update – it's a fundamental change in how our laptops and PCs work. I mean, think about it: your device will be able to learn and adapt to your habits, making it even more personalized and efficient. It's like having a super-smart sidekick built right into your computer.

Nvidia is leading the charge with their plan to reboot your PC using AI. According to The Vergecast, they're working on integrating AI capabilities directly into your laptop. I'm curious to see how this plays out – it's exciting to think about the possibilities. For instance, imagine your laptop anticipating your needs and adjusting its performance accordingly. It's like having a personal assistant that's always one step ahead.

On the other hand, TSMC, the world's biggest chipmaker, is struggling to keep up with AI demand. They're saying it could take a very long time to fulfill customers' needs, even with US-based production. It's a classic problem of supply and demand – and it's holding back the development of more AI-powered devices. My read is that this is a bottleneck that the industry will need to solve if we want to see more AI integration. It's a bit like a traffic jam on the road to AI adoption.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
